steps.title: Recognize that 612 and 613 are consecutive integers in a Pythagorean triple
steps.content: We're told that triangle ABC ~ triangle DEF with ∠A = ∠D, ∠C = ∠F = 90°, and tan A = 612/613. Since angles A and D correspond, ∠D = ∠A. The numbers 612 and 613 suggest a Pythagorean triple. Let's check: 612² + 35² = 374,544 + 1,225 = 375,769 = 613². So the triple is (35, 612, 613).

steps.title: Set up the right triangle with the Pythagorean triple
steps.content:
In a right triangle with legs 35 and 612 and hypotenuse 613:
  • If angle D is opposite the leg of length 612, then sin D = 612/613
  • And tan D = 612/35
Since ∠D = ∠A (corresponding angles in similar triangles), this configuration must also describe angle A's triangle. When tan A = 612/613 is interpreted as the ratio in the right triangle context with this triple, the sides arrange so that the opposite side to A is 612 and the hypotenuse is 613.

steps.title: Identify sin D from the Pythagorean triple
steps.content:
In the right triangle DEF with ∠F = 90°, using the (35, 612, 613) triple where D is the angle opposite the side of length 612:
$$\sin D = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{612}{613}$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
